title: Information Security Policies
description: A set of policies for information security is defined, approved by management, published, and communicated to employees and relevant external parties.

title: Organization of Information Security
description: A framework is established to initiate and control the implementation and operation of information security within the organization.

title: Human Resource Security
description: Employees and contractors understand their responsibilities and are suitable for the roles for which they are considered, with security awareness training.

title: Asset Management
description: Assets associated with information and information processing facilities are identified, inventoried, and assigned ownership.

title: Access Control
description: Access to information and information processing facilities is limited based on business and security requirements.

title: Operations Security
description: Correct and secure operations of information processing facilities are ensured through documented operating procedures.

title: Communications Security
description: Information in networks and supporting information processing facilities is protected through network controls and transfer policies.

title: Incident Management
description: A consistent and effective approach to the management of information security incidents is ensured, including communication and escalation.

title: Business Continuity
description: Information security continuity is embedded in the organization's business continuity management systems.

title: Compliance with Legal Requirements
description: Breaches of legal, statutory, regulatory, or contractual obligations related to information security are avoided.
